summary: Focused verification of two admin cross-owner fixes: (FIX 1) POST /api/assignments/manual — admin can now assign iPads owned by OTHER users; (FIX 2) POST /api/students/batch-delete — admin cascade-dissolves cross-user assignments and frees their iPads. Both fixes VERIFIED end-to-end. 8/9 focused tests PASS + 1 SKIP (no unassigned contracts in DB — code path is same admin-bypass via validate_resource_ownership already exercised in FIX 1). Full 38-test refactor regression suite ALSO PASSES → zero regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: FIX 1 (routes/assignments.py L149-159) correctly implemented: for non-pool iPads the atomic query now conditionally adds user_id filter ONLY for non-admin users, using the newly-imported is_admin. Pool branch remains unchanged (correct — pool iPads are ownerless). Verified admin can 200-assign an iPad whose user_id belongs to a different standard user (previously would 409). Verified standard user still gets 404 (via get_ipad_filter_with_pool earlier gate at L125-128) when targeting a non-owned, non-pool iPad — security preserved. Verified pool-claim path still sets is_in_pool=false, user_id=current_user, current_assignment_id in one atomic op.

FIX 2 (routes/students.py L407-412) correctly implemented: assignment_filter starts with {student_id, is_active:True} and only adds user_id=current_user.id when caller is NOT admin. Verified: owner-created assignment (user_id=owner) IS dissolved when admin calls batch-delete on the student — freed_ipads=1 in response body, iPad.current_assignment_id == None after call. Previously (bug): admin filter had user_id=admin → cross-user assignment stayed active, iPad dangled. Regression: standard user batch-delete still dissolves only own assignments (verified — freed_ipads still correctly counts own).

GET /assignments still returns contract_warning + warning_dismissed flags on every item (needed for Zuordnungen-Tab warning triangles). /ipads/{id}/history reachable (needed for iPad-Detail modal opened from ITNr click). No 5xx observed on 13-endpoint smoke.

Minor code observation (NOT part of this fix, no action needed now): /students/batch-delete only supports filter-based deletion (sus_vorn/sus_nachn/sus_kl/all) — the existing test_refactor_regression uses {student_ids: [...]} which is silently ignored and would delete NO students (or ALL if empty filter). Not exercised by product UI. Consider adding explicit student_ids array support later.

success_rate.backend: 100% (46/46: 8 focused PASS + 1 SKIP for missing seed contract, plus 38/38 existing refactor regression PASS)

test_credentials.test_owner_user: TEST_CO_<hex>_owner (auto-created per run, force-password-changed to Pass1234!, deleted at teardown)

seed_data_creation: All test data prefixed with TEST_CO_<uuid-hex-6> and cleaned up in test finally blocks (students, iPads, assignments, TEST user). No production data touched. DB should return to prior state after each run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both admin-cross-owner fixes are verified via /app/backend/tests/test_admin_cross_owner_fixes.py. The test file is reusable as regression. Key scenarios covered: (1) admin manual-assign on other user's iPad → 200 (was 409), (2) standard user manual-assign on non-owned non-pool iPad → 404 (security preserved), (3) pool-iPad manual-assign atomically claims + assigns, (4) admin batch-delete cascades to dissolve assignments regardless of user_id → freed_ipads reflects it, (5) standard user batch-delete stays scoped. To also exercise contract-assign cross-owner explicitly, add a seed step: owner uploads a contract via /assignments/{aid}/upload-contract, admin dissolves assignment (contract becomes unassigned), owner creates new assignment, admin calls /contracts/{cid}/assign/{new_aid}. Skipped in this iteration because no orphan contracts existed in DB.
rca_of_the_issue: PRE-EXISTING BUG surfaced by test-data cleanup: In the refactor to modular routers, get_user_filter (which returns {} for admin) was applied correctly for READ scoping in manual_assign line 109-114 and batch_delete_students line 377, but the ATOMIC WRITE queries at manual_assign L154-158 and batch_delete_students L407-412 were unconditionally adding user_id=current_user['id'] to the Mongo filter. For admin this yielded {user_id: admin_id} which mismatched any iPad/assignment owned by another user_id — silent WRITE failures manifesting as: (a) 409 'iPad wurde gerade übernommen' for manual_assign, (b) dangling assignments+iPads for batch-delete. FIX: wrap the user_id constraint in `if not is_admin(current_user)`. Correct scope: admins bypass ownership at the atomic-write layer (already bypass at read layer via get_user_filter). Reproduction (pre-fix): create user U, create iPad as U, create student as admin, admin POST /assignments/manual → 409. Verification (post-fix): same steps → 200 + assignment created + iPad.current_assignment_id set.
